routing_rule._doc: v3 router (post-Codex-2026-05-09 [high] fix). Three-way routing with TWO budget gates: (1) IF grep_tokens ≤ 120k, use grep (matches v2); (2) ELIF grep too large AND rtk-err-cat manifest's metadata.rtk_input_truncated=False AND rtk-err-cat output_tokens ≤ 120k (intermediate_budget_tokens), use rtk-err-cat; (3) ELSE use tail (matches v2 fallback). Motivated by §3f's argocd finding that rtk-err-cat scored 0.56 Sonnet vs hybrid-v2's tail at 0.12 on the 89k-line argocd log — but ONLY when rtk's input wasn't silently truncated. Codex 2026-05-08-#2 [high] surfaced rtk's 10 MiB INPUT truncation; Codex 2026-05-09 [high] surfaced that rtk's OUTPUT can also blow past the diagnoser's reasoning budget without being truncated (nodejs at 320k tokens). v3 now gates on BOTH conditions. Same 120k cap on output as the primary grep cap because the empirical Sonnet/Haiku abstain cliff was observed on grep contexts at the same scale — no reason to think rtk-err-cat outputs reason any better at 200k+ than grep outputs do. EVALUATION-TUNED (uses_diagnosis_eval=True): both budgets calibrated against eval_diagnosis_*.json observations. Separate v4 train/holdout would be the cleaner generalization test.

anti_leakage._doc: Same calibration-leakage posture as v2: the 120k threshold was originally selected from eval_diagnosis_*.json abstain-cliff observations. v3's intermediate-routing decision is gated on metadata.rtk_input_truncated, which is a deterministic property of the rtk-err-cat manifest row — not derived from any ground-truth-aware artifact. So v3's INCREMENTAL leakage over v2 is zero (same 120k threshold + a deterministic-flag gate). The CUMULATIVE leakage matches v2: declared True so the lock layer makes it visible. v4 / future work: tune both the budget and the rtk-input-cap threshold against a fresh train/holdout split.
